title: System intent satisfaction: quiet ownership
scope: Governing intent: Stay quiet, repo-native, low-residue, and sharply bounded in ownership.
goal: Assess whether the package avoids becoming visible workflow residue or ambiguous ownership.
non_goals: Do not inventory every shipped payload file.
Do not propose module removals from this review alone.

review_mode.review question: Do ownership and startup surfaces keep the workspace quiet and bounded despite a growing package surface?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: README, module map, ownership docs, startup tiny output, review retention guidance

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task "Make a review each on the satisfaction of the main system intents" --format json; rg ownership/residue/quiet/module-map references

findings.title: Satisfaction is medium-high, with surface growth still the main pressure
findings.summary: Startup is quiet and repo-native, and module ownership is clearer than earlier iterations. However, the number of docs, skills, reviews, and generated references means low-residue depends on compact routers and retention discipline continuing to work.
findings.evidence: README defines lightweight as small checked-in footprint and compact outputs, not zero overhead; start tiny avoided raw file reads; module-map separates workspace, planning, and memory; review README explicitly warns against a second review archive.
findings.risk if unchanged: New useful surfaces can accumulate faster than obsolete surfaces shrink, making the package feel like a visible framework despite compact startup.
findings.suggested action: Do not promote now; use retention and source-payload checks as the enforcement points, and promote only when a concrete surface-growth problem recurs.
findings.promotion trigger: A future audit finds first-line startup/docs requiring agents to read multiple overlapping surfaces for one ordinary decision.

recommendation.defer: Keep watching surface count and first-line read requirements.
recommendation.dismiss: No immediate cleanup issue is supported by this review alone.

retention.trigger: next review-promotion or source-payload audit confirms or supersedes the surface-growth pressure
retention.proof surface: startup output, module map, source-payload checks, review retention records

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task "Make a review each on the satisfaction of the main system intents" --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-08: Review created and filled from durable-intent doctrine pass.
